label: january
quote: It's 4:03 a.m. on a supremely cold January morning and I'm just getting home. I've been out dancing and I'm only half drunk but utterly exhausted.
author: Audrey Niffenegger

label: february
quote: Monday February 6th. '9.36am. Oh god Oh god. Maybe he's fallen in love in New York and stayed there'.
author: Helen Fielding

label: march
quote: Monday 30 March 1668 Up betimes and so to the office there to do business till about 10 o'clock.
author: Samuel Pepys

label: april
quote: It was a bright cold day in April and the clocks were striking thirteen.
author: George Orwell

label: may
quote: Left Munich at 8.35 p.m. on 1st May arriving at Vienna early the next morning.
author: Bram Stoker

label: june
quote: June 13 1990. Thirty-seven minutes past two in the morning. And sixteen seconds.
author: Stephen King

label: july
quote: At 7.29 in the morning of 1 July the cinematographer finds himself filming silence itself.
author: Elizabeth Speller

label: august
quote: Today was Tuesday the fifteenth of August; the sun had risen at eleven minutes past five this morning and would set at two minutes before seven this evening.
author: Donald E Westlake

label: september
quote: Just after five o'clock on this chill September morning the fishmonger's cart containing Kirsten and Emilia and such possessions as they have been allowed to bring passed under the city gate.
author: Rose Tremain

label: october
quote: 23 October 1893 3.39am. Upon further thought I feel it necessary to explain that exile into the Master's workshop is not entirely a hardship.
author: William Jablonsky

label: november
quote: I quite agree with you, said Mr Murbles. It is a most awkward situation. Lady Dormer died at precisely 10.37 a.m. on November 10th.
book: The Unpleasantness at the Bellona Club
author: Dorothy L. Sayers

label: december
quote: It was 5:02 a.m. December 14. In another fifty-eight minutes he would set sail for America. He did not want to leave his wife.
author: Brenda Joyce
